questions.q: A random experiment has two possible outcomes, namely A and B. What is the probability of A?
questions.answer: The probability of A is 1/2 as the outcomes are equally likely.

questions.q: A coin is tossed. What is the probability of getting a head?
questions.answer: The probability of getting a head is 1/2 as the outcomes are equally likely.

questions.q: A die is rolled. What is the probability of getting a number greater than 4?
questions.answer: The probability of getting a number greater than 4 is 1/2 as there are 3 favorable outcomes out of 6 possible outcomes.

questions.q: A box contains 5 red marbles, 8 white marbles, and 4 black marbles. What is the probability of drawing a red marble at random?
questions.answer: The probability of drawing a red marble is 5/17 as there are 5 favorable outcomes out of 17 possible outcomes.

questions.q: A bag contains 3 blue balls and 2 red balls. A ball is drawn at random. What is the probability that it is blue?
questions.answer: The probability that the ball is blue is 3/5 as there are 3 favorable outcomes out of 5 possible outcomes.

questions.q: A random experiment has three possible outcomes, namely E1, E2, and E3. What is the probability that at least one of these events occurs?
questions.answer: The probability that at least one of these events occurs is 1 as the events are exhaustive.

questions.q: A coin is tossed twice. What is the probability that at least one head occurs?
questions.answer: The probability that at least one head occurs is 3/4 as there are 3 favorable outcomes out of 4 possible outcomes.

questions.q: A die is rolled three times. What is the probability that at least one 6 occurs?
questions.answer: The probability that at least one 6 occurs is 1- (probability of no 6 occurring) = 1- (5/6)^3 = 91/216.

questions.q: A random experiment has two possible outcomes, namely A and B. What is the probability that exactly one of these events occurs?
questions.answer: The probability that exactly one of these events occurs is P(A) + P(B) - 2P(A ∩ B) = 1/2 + 1/2 - 2(1/2)(1/2) = 1/2.

questions.q: A bag contains 3 blue balls and 2 red balls. A ball is drawn at random. What is the probability that it is not blue?
questions.answer: The probability that it is not blue is 2/5 as there are 2 favorable outcomes out of 5 possible outcomes.

questions.q: A die is rolled. What is the probability that the number shown is even?
questions.answer: The probability that the number shown is even is 3/6 = 1/2 as there are 3 favorable outcomes out of 6 possible outcomes.

questions.q: A coin is tossed three times. What is the probability that the number of heads is odd?
questions.answer: The probability that the number of heads is odd is 3/8 as there are 3 favorable outcomes out of 8 possible outcomes.

questions.q: A random experiment has three possible outcomes, namely E1, E2, and E3. What is the probability that exactly two of these events occur?
questions.answer: The probability that exactly two of these events occur is P(E1 ∩ E2) + P(E2 ∩ E3) + P(E3 ∩ E1) = 3P(E1)P(E2) + 3P(E2)P(E3) + 3P(E3)P(E1) = 3(1/2)(1/2) = 3/4 as the events are pairwise independent.

questions.q: A bag contains 3 blue balls and 2 red balls. A ball is drawn at random. What is the probability that it is either blue or red?
questions.answer: The probability that it is either blue or red is 1 as the events are exhaustive.
